summary: Russian forces killed a Ukrainian evacuation team near Huliaipole with FPV drones despite a declared ceasefire, while Ukrainian FPV drones destroyed a Russian UAZ Bukhanka, killing at least two troops. The Rubicon Center reported intercepting over 2,000 Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, with operators destroying 110 targets on April 11 and 127 on April 10, including coordination with Russian Aerospace Forces. Ukrainian forces survived an FPV strike near Kostiantynivka, using drone detectors to anticipate the attack, as Russian soldiers attempted to surrender before fleeing and being killed by drones. Meanwhile, Ukraine’s XDOWN unveiled the STUD drone, a 300 km/h, 64 km-range FPV drone with an 800-gram warhead, while enemy forces reduced FPV control frequency and increased REB antenna sizes.
